data.title: Hong Kong aims to be top fixed income hub with SFC and HKMA unveiling plan to boost demand, liquidity, and innovation.
data.description: The Securities and Futures Commission (SFC) and the Hong Kong Monetary Authority (HKMA) have announced a comprehensive plan aimed at positioning Hong Kong as a leading center for fixed income and currency markets worldwide. This initiative is designed to boost demand, enhance liquidity, and promote innovation in these markets. The detailed roadmap outlines key strategies to achieve this goal, highlighting the importance of establishing a strong presence in the global financial landscape. This development is expected to attract significant investment and propel Hong Kong's financial sector to new heights in terms of market growth and competitiveness.
Delegates walk out of General Assembly.

data.title: Cushman & Wakefield (NYSE: CWK) to release Q3 2025 earnings on Oct 30 at 7:00 a.m. ET, with a conference call following at 9:00 a.m. ET.
data.description: Cushman & Wakefield (NYSE: CWK) is set to announce its third quarter 2025 financial results on October 30, 2025, at around 7:00 a.m. ET. Following the release, management will conduct a conference call at 9:00 a.m. ET on the same day to delve into the financial outcomes. Participants can join the call by dialing 1-833-821-5374 for domestic callers or 1-412-652-1260 for international callers. Alternatively, a clickable link will be available 15 minutes before the conference call.

data.title: Chatham Lodging Trust increases unsecured credit facility with refinancing in West Palm Beach.
data.description: In West Palm Beach, Florida, hospitality company Chatham Lodging Trust has successfully refinanced and upsized its unsecured credit facility. The refinancing effort saw an increase in the credit facility to an undisclosed amount, signifying improved financial health and prospects for the organization. This move is indicative of the company's strategic financial planning and commitment to growth within the hospitality sector.

data.title: Global education construction projects valued at $322.8 billion; North America leads with $124.8 billion, followed by Western Europe, MENA, and NE Asia.
data.description: An analysis report titled "Project Insight - Global Education Construction Projects (Q3 2025)" has been recently included in ResearchAndMarkets.com's offerings. The report covers education construction projects worldwide, from initial pre-planning phases (such as announcement and study) to implementation. The total value of these projects is estimated at $322.8 billion. The report offers detailed insights based on the analyst's assessment of construction projects, including the total value of projects, analysis categorized by stage of development, and funding sources across all regions.

data.title: Cloudflare partners with Giga to boost school connectivity; using Speed Test solution to monitor global school internet access.
data.description: In San Francisco, Cloudflare, Inc. (NYSE: NET) revealed a fresh collaboration with Giga, a venture by UNICEF and the International Telecommunication Union (ITU), focused on aiding Governments in linking all schools to the web. Giga will now leverage Cloudflare's Speed Test tool for up-to-the-minute data to enhance the assessment and supervision of internet connectivity in schools worldwide. This initiative addresses the crucial need to assist governments in this mission.

data.title: Swish receives seed funding for product sampling, led by BD Ventures, to offer full-size CPG products through grocery shopping.
data.description: A Detroit-based company called Swish, known for its programmatic product trial services, has successfully completed a seed funding round. The investment was led by BD Ventures and also saw participation from a cluster of strategic retail technology and shopper marketing investors. Swish's concept revolves around offering customers full-size consumer packaged goods (CPG) products as samples during their routine grocery shopping trips. The platform utilizes advanced AI technology to analyze real transaction data, ensuring the samples are relevant to each shopper and delivering a personalized experience. The amount of funds raised during the seed round has not been disclosed, but the involvement of notable investors highlights confidence in Swish's innovative approach to product trials.

data.title: TurningPoint Energy and Pivot Energy launch 1st of 16.6MW solar project in Baltimore, Maryland.
data.description: In Baltimore, TurningPoint Energy and Pivot Energy have introduced a new solar project in the region, marking the beginning of a 16.6MW portfolio in Maryland.

data.title: Lamb Weston to release Q1 earnings; analysts expect 53 cents/share, down from 73 cents. Annual dividend yield is 2.80%.
data.description: Lamb Weston Holdings, Inc. (NYSE: LW) is set to announce its first-quarter earnings on Tuesday, Sept. 30, before the market opens. Analysts are anticipating earnings per share of 53 cents, a decline from 73 cents in the same period last year. The company is expected to report quarterly revenue of $1.62 billion, slightly lower than the $1.65 billion generated in the previous year. JP Morgan analyst Thomas Palmer initiated coverage on Lamb Weston with a Neutral rating and a price target of $57 on Aug. 20. Lamb Weston currently offers an annual dividend yield of 2.80%, equivalent to a quarterly dividend of 37 cents per share ($1.48 annually). Investors are closely watching the company for potential gains from dividends.

data.title: American Rebel Light Beer sponsors Tony Stewart's Top Fuel Dragster, Matt Hagan's Funny Car, and John Hall's Pro Stock Motorcycle at NHRA Midwest Nationals on September 26 – 28.
data.description: A recent report reveals that the global unemployment rate has reached 5.7%, with approximately 190 million people currently jobless worldwide. It further noted that youth unemployment stands at 13.1%, affecting around 64 million individuals. Moreover, the report highlighted that the COVID-19 pandemic has significantly contributed to the rise in unemployment rates across various countries. The situation is particularly dire in developing nations, where limited access to vaccines and economic resources pose additional challenges for job seekers. Experts emphasize the urgent need for coordinated efforts to address the job crisis and prevent further economic instability.
